name: admin-triggered-payments-let-20260410-1448
idea: Admin-triggered payments: let the admin mention the bot in a channel to create a payment for a Banodoco user, collect/store the user's Solana wallet privately if needed, send a test payment, use the user's confirmation message to authorize the final payout, and keep all confirmation and money-moving checks fail-closed.

overrides.note: User clarified v1: do NOT require wallet_address in the admin tool call. If no verified Solana wallet is on file for the Banodoco user, create the payment request intent, ping the intended recipient in the originating channel/thread, ask them to reply with their wallet there, and continue only from that recipient's reply. DM path is explicitly deferred for now. Final-payment confirmation should also rely on the intended recipient's message/confirmation in-channel, not DM.

Revision lock: multiple recipients may have concurrent payment intents in the same channel or thread. Intent matching must be deterministic and identity-gated first by guild_id + source_channel/thread + recipient_user_id + active status before any agent classification runs. The agent should only classify semantics (wallet provided / positive confirmation / ambiguous / negative / strange behavior) after a single candidate intent is identified. Any ambiguity, conflicting signals, suspicious behavior, or out-of-the-ordinary message pattern must fail closed and explicitly notify/tag the admin for review rather than advancing payout.

Hard revision required before execution: replace in-memory pending intents with a persisted admin_payment_intents table; replace amount_usd-first admin flow with amount_sol/requested_amount_sol as the canonical admin-entered amount; add restart-safe free-text wallet/confirmation recovery using persisted message cursor fields (for example last_scanned_message_id and resolved_by_message_id) plus reconciliation on startup/interval; keep deterministic identity/context gating first and allow multiple concurrent intents in the same thread/channel; the agent may classify semantics only after a single candidate intent is selected; any ambiguity or strange behavior must fail closed and tag the admin; button confirmation may remain as a secondary path but the plan must include free-text confirmation with recovery because the user explicitly asked for it.

last_gate.rationale: The plan is now fundamentally aligned with the user's v1 requirements: no wallet_address in the tool call, persisted admin_payment_intents table, in-channel wallet collection, deterministic identity gating, free-text confirmation as primary path, fail-closed on ambiguity. The remaining 5 open flags reduce to 2 distinct concerns (button-intent sync and Anthropic client bootstrap), both of which are straightforward to resolve during execution. The button-intent gap is already naturally covered: `_handoff_terminal_result` (payment_cog.py:269-299) fires for ALL terminal payment states regardless of confirmation path, so `handle_payment_result` will update the intent row whether the user clicked the button or typed free-text. The Anthropic client concern is an implementation detail — the interceptor can instantiate its own lightweight `anthropic.AsyncAnthropic` or call `_ensure_agent()` before classification. Neither concern requires architectural redesign. A third iteration would be churn.
last_gate.signals_assessment: Iteration 2, weighted score 24.5 (up from 21.5). The score increase is misleading — it reflects new flags replacing old ones, not degradation. The 10 critical v1-alignment flags from iteration 1 (requirement divergence, missing infrastructure, wrong success criteria) are all resolved by the plan redesign. The remaining 5 open flags are 2 distinct implementation-detail concerns: button-intent state sync and Anthropic client initialization for the interceptor. Both have clear solutions verified against the codebase. Preflight passes. No recurring critiques. No debt overlap with escalated subsystems. Plan delta was 86% (near-total rewrite), which is appropriate given the fundamental design change required.
warnings: The executor must ensure handle_payment_result updates the intent row to 'confirmed'/'completed' on final-payment terminal states — this is what closes the button-intent sync gap. Without this, startup recovery would re-scan for already-confirmed intents.

The interceptor's agent classification needs its own Anthropic client initialization (either a standalone AsyncAnthropic instance or calling _ensure_agent() first). Do not assume self.agent is initialized when _check_pending_payment_reply fires.
The SQL migration file must be applied to Supabase before the code deploys — coordinate with the deployment process.

settled_decisions.decision: Producer name is 'admin_chat' — auto-discovered by PaymentCog._candidate_producer_cog_names without modifying PaymentCog.
settled_decisions.rationale: Verified: _candidate_producer_cog_names('admin_chat') generates ['AdminChatCog', ...] which matches the cog class name.

settled_decisions.decision: handle_payment_result is the single convergence point for both button and free-text confirmation paths — it updates the intent row to terminal status.
settled_decisions.rationale: Verified at payment_cog.py:251-299: _handoff_terminal_result fires for ALL terminal states regardless of confirmation method. This naturally syncs the intent row without needing to modify PaymentConfirmView.

settled_decisions.decision: Admin must initiate from guild channel, not DM. DM initiation fails closed.
settled_decisions.rationale: AdminChatCog does not resolve guild context for admin DMs, and there is no channel to ping the recipient in. Guild-only is the correct constraint for v1.

settled_decisions.decision: The interceptor uses its own lightweight Anthropic client for classification, independent of the full AdminChatAgent.
settled_decisions.rationale: The Anthropic client is just AsyncAnthropic(api_key=...) with no agent-specific state. A standalone instance avoids coupling interceptor availability to agent initialization order.

last_gate.orchestrator_guidance: Plan passed gate and preflight. Proceed to finalize. Verify unresolved flags against the plan and project code before accepting.
